Question: If $$y$$ varies directly as $$x$$ and $$y = 3 , \text{when} x = 10 .$$ find the equation that relates x and y .
o3 Answer:
Because “y varies directly as x,” y is proportional to x:

 y = k x  (k is the constant of variation)

Use the given pair (x, y) = (10, 3) to find k:

 3 = k(10)  
 k = 3/10 = 0.3

Substitute k back:

 y = (3/10)x  (or equivalently 10y = 3x)

Thus the equation relating x and y is  

 y = (3/10)x.
